title: crocanthemum propinquum
intro: Crocanthemum propinquum, also known as tickseed or whiteflower, is a shrub-like plant native to eastern North America, belonging to the sunflower family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Crocanthemum propinquum include its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with herbal preparations often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to promote overall well-being.
